title: Fantastic Padova
subtitle: A Fantastic Guide to Padova
author: Grazia Guarnieri
description: Fantastic Padova is a guide that does not tell you where to go, but how to look. Through legends, forgotten stories, silent architecture and the dreamlike illustrations of Giulia Lazzaron, the book reveals a hidden city: water Padova, feminine Padova, esoteric Padova. A city built on canals, traversed by saints, demons, scholars, Trojan refugees and ultramarine blue skies.

editorial_manifesto.title: A city as threshold
editorial_manifesto.description: The Fantastic Guides by Peacock do not describe places: they build perceptual states. Fantastic Padova transforms the city into a narrative organism made of water, memory, silences and apparitions. Every place is a threshold, every detail a fragment of an emotional geography.

narrative_threads.title: The invisible women of Padova
narrative_threads.description: Four female figures move through the book like underground presences. Women who changed European cultural history and whom Padova keeps almost in silence.

figures.name: Giulia Bigolina
figures.title: The first Italian woman novelist
figures.description: A Padovan noblewoman and author of Urania (1554), considered the first prose novel written by a woman in Western history. The manuscript remained hidden for centuries in the Biblioteca Trivulziana in Milan. Her writing anticipates feminist and identity themes by four centuries.

figures.name: Elena Lucrezia Corner Piscopia
figures.title: The first woman to graduate in the world
figures.description: On 25 June 1678 she graduated in philosophy at the University of Padova. The ceremony attracted thousands of people and was moved to the Cathedral because the Palazzo del Bo could not contain them. Her figure transforms Padova into a European symbol of female knowledge.

figures.name: Beata Eustochio
figures.title: The mystical presence
figures.description: A spiritual figure linked to the religious memory of the city. In the book she appears as a silent, almost suspended presence, inhabiting the contemplative and invisible Padova.

figures.name: Traversina Cortellieri
figures.title: The woman who commissioned a chapel
figures.description: In the fourteenth century she commissions a chapel at the Eremitani church. A rare gesture for a woman of that era, transforming her presence into an architectural trace that survived the centuries.

narrative_threads.title: The city built on water
narrative_threads.description: Padova emerges as a fluvial organism. The canals are not decorative elements but veins of the city: invisible paths that shape memory, trade, silences and reflections.

narrative_threads.title: Saints, demons and thresholds
narrative_threads.description: Carved portals, hidden symbols, religious figures and unsettling presences move through the city. Fantastic Padova reads architecture as a symbolic language.

key_concepts.name: A guide that is not a guide
key_concepts.description: The book does not propose tourist itineraries but perceptual devices. Fantastic Padova invites the reader to slow down and observe the city as a mental space.

key_concepts.name: Padova, city of water
key_concepts.description: Water is the secret grammar of the city. Naviglio Interno, Piovego and Brentella build a liquid geography that survives beneath the contemporary urban landscape.

key_concepts.name: The blue vault of the Scrovegni
key_concepts.description: Giotto's sky in the Scrovegni Chapel: golden stars suspended over ultramarine blue made from lapis lazuli. A blue that connects Padova to Afghanistan, medieval painting and memory.

key_concepts.name: Antenore and the Trojan city
key_concepts.description: According to legend, Padova was founded by Antenore, a Trojan refugee fleeing after the war. The city is thus read as a place of arrival, diaspora and memory.

key_places.name: Scrovegni Chapel
key_places.description: Giotto's blue sky as immersive and metaphysical experience.

key_places.name: Palazzo del Bo
key_places.description: Symbolic heart of the University of Padova.

key_places.name: Basilica of Sant'Antonio
key_places.description: Spiritual and popular hub of the city.

key_places.name: Chiesa degli Eremitani
key_places.description: Space traversed by feminine and artistic memory.

key_places.name: Prato della Valle
key_places.description: Large elliptical space as urban theatre and water mirror.

key_places.name: Historic canals
key_places.description: Naviglio Interno, Piovego and Brentella as the invisible structure of the city.

central_tension: Padova is a city that changed European history — and often forgets its own radicalism. First modern university, first woman to graduate in the world, first Italian female novel: the book returns to the city the memory of what it has been.

catalog_relations.title: Blu Oltremare
catalog_relations.author: Emmanuelle Filizzola
catalog_relations.relation: The blue of the Scrovegni directly dialogues with the chromatic and symbolic journey of the novel.

catalog_relations.title: Fantastic Turin
catalog_relations.relation: Shares the narrative format of the Fantastic Guides: cities read as symbolic landscapes.

catalog_relations.title: Frequenze Glossary
catalog_relations.relation: Fantastic Padova applies concepts such as ma, yugen, basho and lentezza to urban reading.

manifesto_quote: There are no invisible cities. There are only cities we have stopped looking at.
